description: Where groundwater reaches the surface naturally, the source place is a 'thula'. Flow and water chemistry vary with season and geology. 'phitura' (well) reaches groundwater through a made opening, while 'phuliro' (stream) is the watercourse that a spring may begin or feed.
articulatory_notes: Rest the tongue against the upper teeth for unvoiced 'th' and let the breath continue into stressed high-back rounded 'u'. Touch the tongue tip to the alveolar ridge for voiced lateral 'l', release the voice around its sides, and lower the tongue into central 'a'.

pillars.solarpunk-values: Protecting a spring brings groundwater and land use to one visible source. Access, habitat, and downstream flow meet there too. The word makes the place easy to name before those relations are discussed.
pillars.pre-industrial-wisdom: Finding and tending a spring depends on local geology and weather. Seasonal flow, animals, and signs of contamination are learned at the same place. A reliable source is learned over time.

semantic_domains.nature: a natural point where groundwater reaches the surface
semantic_domains.spatial: a source place from which surface water may begin to flow
